zaru-soba-cold-buckwheat-noodles.name: Zaru Soba (cold Buckwheat Noodles)

ingredients.prep: thinly sliced scallions, shredded toasted nori, and/or wasabi (for serving; optional)

instructions: Bring 3 Tbsp. mirin (sweet Japanese rice wine), 3 Tbsp. soy sauce, 1 Tbsp. instant dashi powder (such as Hondashi), and 3 cups water to a boil in a small saucepan. Reduce heat so mixture is at a simmer and cook until flavors come together, about 4 minutes. Let mentsuyu cool, then transfer to an airtight container. Cover and chill until ready to use.

Meanwhile, cook 6 oz. dried soba noodles (about 2 bundles) in a medium pot of boiling water according to package instructions (about 4 minutes is typically just right). Drain in a colander, then rinse under cold running water, taking care to remove any residual starch. Let sit 5 minutes.

Divide noodles between 2 bowls. Ladle chilled mentsuyu into 2 smaller bowls and add an ice cube or two for an extra refreshing broth. Serve noodles topped with finely grated daikon, thinly sliced scallions, shredded toasted nori, and/or wasabi if desired. Dip a small amount of noodles in mentsuyu at a time, adding more ice cubes as you go as needed.

zucchini-roll-with-sucuk-meatballs-lezze.name: Zucchini Roll With Sucuk Meatballs
instructions: 1. Clean the ends of the grapes by scraping their uniforms.
2. Put it up to soften in the oven or rub it light.

19. Cook and cook for 10 minutes.

21. Add classic pumpkin meatballs recipe among your notes.

zuppa-di-pesce-fra-di-avolo.name: Zuppa Di Pesce Fra Di Avolo
instructions: Heat 3 tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat; cook and stir sea scallops, shrimp, and calamari rings in the hot oil until scallops and calamari are opaque and shrimp are pink, about 5 minutes.
Bring a separate pot of water to a boil and add mussels and littleneck clams to the boiling water; cook until shells have opened, about 3 minutes.
Remove shellfish and rinse with hot water.
Pour crushed tomatoes, water, 2 cans chopped clams, 1/4 cup olive oil, onion, red pepper flakes, garlic, oregano, parsley, and salt into a large pot and bring to a boil.
Stir cooked scallops, shrimp, calamari, mussels in shells, and littleneck clams in shells into tomato mixture. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.

About 10 minutes before serving, bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil; cook the linguine at a boil until tender yet firm to the bite, about 10 minutes; drain and rinse pasta in hot water to prevent sticking.
Serve seafood and sauce over pasta.
